perturbation.original_text: 2. Use and Disclosure of Confidential Information . 2.1 The Recipient agrees that all Confidential Information will be held and maintained by it in the strictest confidence, will be used by it solely and exclusively for the purpose of evaluating, negotiating and implementing a Restructuring, and will not, directly or indirectly, be used or disclosed by it for any other purpose whatsoever. CEDC acknowledges that Recipient is engaged in businesses similar to those conducted by CEDC and the CEDC Group or other industries in the ordinary course of Recipient’s business. The Recipient will use reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality of Confidential Information, provided that such care shall be at least as great as the precautions taken by the Recipient to protect its own confidential information of similar nature.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Use and Disclosure of Confidential Information . 2.1 The Recipient agrees that all Confidential Information *as it deems appropriate* will be held and maintained by it in the strictest confidence, will be used by it solely and exclusively for the purpose of evaluating, negotiating and implementing a Restructuring, and will not, directly or indirectly, be used or disclosed by it for any other purpose whatsoever. CEDC acknowledges that Recipient is engaged in businesses similar to those conducted by CEDC and the CEDC Group or other industries in the ordinary course of Recipient’s business. The Recipient will use reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality of Confidential Information, provided that such care shall be at least as great as the precautions taken by the Recipient to protect its own confidential information of similar nature.
perturbation.explanation: The change, inserting "as it deems appropriate", gives the Recipient discretionary power over what constitutes Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the original intention of the agreement, where CEDC defines and protects its information. This undermines the entire purpose of the NDA. The change directly conflicts with section 1.1 and 1.2 where Confidential Information is defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Scope of Confidential Information . 1.1 Subject to Section 1.2 below, “ Confidential Information ” means confidential, secret, proprietary or other non-public information pertaining to the business, operations, brands, marketing plans, financial matters, legal matters, products, projects, business plans or practices, research and development, product development, intellectual property, financial models, trademarks, trade secrets, accounting and financing data, and methods of production, distribution or procurement, suppliers, distributors, consultants, advisors or employees, directors or officers of CEDC or any of its Subsidiaries (defined below) (together, the “ CEDC Group ”) that is disclosed or otherwise made available, either orally or in writing, by any member of the CEDC Group to the Recipient or its affiliates, agents, or advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, attorneys, banks and other sources of equity and debt financing and accountants) (collectively, “ Representatives ”).
perturbation.justification: The change, inserting "as it deems appropriate", allows the Recipient to subjectively determine what constitutes Confidential Information, contradicting the defined scope of Confidential Information in Section 1.1. Section 1.1 specifies that Confidential Information includes a wide range of information pertaining to the business of CEDC, leaving no room for the Recipient to subjectively decide what is confidential. The change therefore directly contradicts the original intent and defined terms, resulting in a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2.2 The Recipient agrees that it will not, without the prior written consent of CEDC, directly or indirectly, disclose all or any portion of the Confidential Information, or the substance thereof, to any third party other than its Representatives in connection with the Purpose, except, subject to Section 2.3 below, to the extent required by applicable law or legal process.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.2 The Recipient agrees that it *may*, without the prior written consent of CEDC, directly or indirectly, disclose all or any portion of the Confidential Information, or the substance thereof, to any third party other than its Representatives in connection with the Purpose, except, subject to Section 2.3 below, to the extent required by applicable law or legal process.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “will not” to “may” completely reverses the obligation regarding disclosure of Confidential Information. The original text strictly prohibits disclosure without CEDC’s consent, while the modified text allows it. This creates a direct contradiction that makes the entire purpose of confidentiality unclear and unenforceable. This contradicts section 2.1's insistence on maintaining all Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Use and Disclosure of Confidential Information . 2.1 The Recipient agrees that all Confidential Information will be held and maintained by it in the strictest confidence, will be used by it solely and exclusively for the purpose of evaluating, negotiating and implementing a Restructuring, and will not, directly or indirectly, be used or disclosed by it for any other purpose whatsoever. CEDC acknowledges that Recipient is engaged in businesses similar to those conducted by CEDC and the CEDC Group or other industries in the ordinary course of Recipient’s business. The Recipient will use reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality of Confidential Information, provided that such care shall be at least as great as the precautions taken by the Recipient to protect its own confidential information of similar nature.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters 'will not' to 'may', completely reversing the obligation concerning disclosure of Confidential Information. The original text prohibits disclosure without consent, while the changed text permits it. This directly contradicts section 2.1, which mandates that all Confidential Information be held in the strictest confidence and not be disclosed for any other purpose. This creates a clear and strong contradiction, making the confidentiality terms unenforceable, therefore it's a structural flaw as explained in the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2 The Recipient agrees that, upon written request by CEDC, all Confidential Information (and all copies, summaries and notes of the contents or parts thereof) and all Company property received by the Recipient shall, as soon as reasonably practicable, be either returned to CEDC or, to the extent technically practicable, destroyed (at the Recipient’s or its Representatives’ option), except that Recipient and its Representatives may retain copies of Confidential Information as is required to comply with applicable law or regulation or professional standards or internal compliance requirements. The Recipient’s obligations under Section 2 hereof shall survive the return of such tangible embodiments of Confidential Information until termination of this Agreement. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, neither Recipient nor any of its Representatives shall be required to delete electronically stored Confidential Information to the extent such deletion would be technologically impracticable or inconsistent with the archival records retention policy of the Recipient or its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 The Recipient agrees that, upon written request by CEDC, all Confidential Information (and all copies, summaries and notes of the contents or parts thereof) and all Company property received by the Recipient shall, *at its sole discretion*, be either returned to CEDC or, to the extent technically practicable, destroyed (at the Recipient’s or its Representatives’ option), except that Recipient and its Representatives may retain copies of Confidential Information as is required to comply with applicable law or regulation or professional standards or internal compliance requirements. The Recipient’s obligations under Section 2 hereof shall survive the return of such tangible embodiments of Confidential Information until termination of this Agreement. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, neither Recipient nor any of its Representatives shall be required to delete electronically stored Confidential Information to the extent such deletion would be technologically impracticable or inconsistent with the archival records retention policy of the Recipient or its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states the Recipient must return or destroy confidential information upon request. Inserting "at its sole discretion" gives the Recipient the option to ignore the request, contradicting the obligation to return or destroy. The change directly clashes with the rest of the clause requiring the Recipient to either return or destroy the mentioned Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 The Recipient agrees that, upon written request by CEDC, all Confidential Information (and all copies, summaries and notes of the contents or parts thereof) and all Company property received by the Recipient shall, at its sole discretion, be either returned to CEDC or, to the extent technically practicable, destroyed (at the Recipient’s or its Representatives’ option), except that Recipient and its Representatives may retain copies of Confidential Information as is required to comply with applicable law or regulation or professional standards or internal compliance requirements. The Recipient’s obligations under Section 2 hereof shall survive the return of such tangible embodiments of Confidential Information until termination of this Agreement. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, neither Recipient nor any of its Representatives shall be required to delete electronically stored Confidential Information to the extent such deletion would be technologically impracticable or inconsistent with the archival records retention policy of the Recipient or its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text obligates the Recipient to return or destroy confidential information upon request by CEDC. The changed text adds "at its sole discretion", which allows the Recipient to choose whether or not to comply with the request, directly contradicting the obligation to return or destroy as it gives them the option to ignore CEDC's request entirely, creating a direct conflict within section 3.2 as the Contradicted Text displays.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Termination. 7.1 The provisions of this Agreement shall continue in full force and effect at all times until the earliest to occur of (i) the filing of an exchange offer, offering memorandum or disclosure statement with respect to the 2016 Notes or Convertible Notes, provided that , to the extent CEDC and the Recipient continue discussions on the terms of a Restructuring following such filing, this period shall extend until the filing of an amended exchange offer, offering memorandum or disclosure statement reflecting such discussions or (ii) 9:00 a.m. (GMT) on March 5, 2013, whereupon this Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force or effect (the “ Termination Date ”), other than with respect to the rights and obligations of CEDC and Recipient per Section 7.2 hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Termination. 7.1 The provisions of this Agreement shall continue in full force and effect at all times *unless the Recipient deems the information no longer confidential*, until the earliest to occur of (i) the filing of an exchange offer, offering memorandum or disclosure statement with respect to the 2016 Notes or Convertible Notes, provided that , to the extent CEDC and the Recipient continue discussions on the terms of a Restructuring following such filing, this period shall extend until the filing of an amended exchange offer, offering memorandum or disclosure statement reflecting such discussions or (ii) 9:00 a.m. (GMT) on March 5, 2013, whereupon this Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force or effect (the “ Termination Date ”), other than with respect to the rights and obligations of CEDC and Recipient per Section 7.2 hereof.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly defines the conditions under which the NDA terminates. The added clause, "unless the Recipient deems the information no longer confidential," introduces a subjective and unilateral termination condition, directly contradicting the pre-defined termination dates. The added clause gives the Recipient complete control over the NDA's duration, undermining the enforceability of the entire agreement. This contradicts the defined termination date and the purpose of Section 7.2 related to the 'Cleansing Document'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Termination. 7.1 The provisions of this Agreement shall continue in full force and effect at all times unless the Recipient deems the information no longer confidential, until the earliest to occur of (i) the filing of an exchange offer, offering memorandum or disclosure statement with respect to the 2016 Notes or Convertible Notes, provided that , to the extent CEDC and the Recipient continue discussions on the terms of a Restructuring following such filing, this period shall extend until the filing of an amended exchange offer, offering memorandum or disclosure statement reflecting such discussions or (ii) 9:00 a.m. (GMT) on March 5, 2013, whereupon this Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force or effect (the “ Termination Date ”), other than with respect to the rights and obligations of CEDC and Recipient per Section 7.2 hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause that allows the Recipient to unilaterally terminate the agreement if they deem the information no longer confidential. This directly contradicts the originally defined termination dates (March 5, 2013, or upon filing of an exchange offer), creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text contains the modified text as well because the contradiction lies in the addition of a clause that conflicts with the original terms, and this addition is present in the contradicted text.
